source_jobs.title: Senior DevOps Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ASPORA




People on the move deserve a bank that moves with them. Since 2022, Aspora has been building a borderless financial operating system that makes money as mobile and transparent as its users.

Backed by influential venture capitalists like Sequoia Capital, Greylock Partners, Hummingbird Ventures, Y Combinator & Global Founders Capital. We're a team of 150+ across India, the UK, the UAE, EU and the US, working with extreme ownership, radical candour, and an obsession with customer impact.

We celebrate builders who question assumptions, ship fast, and turn regulatory complexity into elegant solutions. If you’re driven to redefine what global banking can be, we’d love to build the future with you.


ABOUT THE ROLE

At Aspora, infrastructure isn't an afterthought — it's a competitive advantage. As a DevOps Engineer, you will own the reliability, scalability, and security of the systems that power financial transactions for customers across multiple countries. You'll work at the intersection of engineering and operations, enabling our product and backend teams to ship fast without breaking things.

This role sits within the Engineering team and reports directly to the Engineering Lead. You'll be a foundational hire on our infrastructure function, with the opportunity to define tooling, standards, and culture from the ground up.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

 - Design, build, and maintain CI/CD pipelines to enable fast, reliable, and automated deployments across environments

 - Manage and evolve our cloud infrastructure on AWS (and/or GCP), including networking, compute, storage, and security configurations

 - Implement and maintain Infrastructure as Code (IaC) using Terraform or Pulumi, ensuring reproducible and version-controlled environments

 - Own observability: set up and manage monitoring, alerting, and logging using tools such as Datadog, Grafana, or Prometheus

 - Collaborate closely with backend, mobile, and security engineers to ensure infrastructure supports product velocity and compliance requirements

 - Lead incident response, post-mortems, and drive improvements to system reliability and uptime

 - Enforce security best practices including secrets management, IAM policies, vulnerability scanning, and compliance controls (PCI-DSS awareness a plus)

 - Manage containerised workloads using Docker and Kubernetes (EKS or GKE)

 - Support developer tooling, internal environments, and platform engineering initiatives


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 4–6 years of hands-on DevOps or SRE experience in a cloud-native environment

 - Strong proficiency with AWS (EC2, RDS, S3, IAM, VPC, Lambda); GCP experience is a plus

 - Solid experience with Kubernetes — managing clusters, deployments, autoscaling, and networking

 - Expertise in CI/CD tooling: GitHub Actions, CircleCI, ArgoCD, or equivalent

 - Infrastructure as Code experience with Terraform (Pulumi or CDK also acceptable)

 - Comfort working in a Linux/Unix environment with strong any scripting skills.

 - Experience with observability stacks: Datadog, Grafana, Prometheus, or similar.

 - Strong security mindset — you understand secrets management, IAM, and network hardening

 - Ability to thrive in a fast-paced, ambiguous startup environment with a high ownership bias


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience in fintech, payments, or regulated industries (PCI-DSS, SOC 2)

 - Familiarity with service mesh technologies (Istio, Linkerd)

 - Exposure to multi-region or multi-cloud deployments

 - Experience with database operations (RDS, PostgreSQL, Redis) including backups and failover

 - Prior experience in a high-growth Series A/B startup


WHY JOIN ASPORA

 - Build infrastructure that powers global money movement across 5+ countries

 - High ownership, high impact — you will shape our platform function from an early stage

 - Work with a globally distributed, high-calibre team across India, UK, UAE, and the US

 - Competitive compensation, benefits, and ESOPs

 - A culture built

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ASPORA




People on the move deserve a bank that moves with them. Since 2022, Aspora has been building a borderless financial operating system that makes money as mobile and transparent as its users.

Backed by influential venture capitalists like Sequoia Capital, Greylock Partners, Hummingbird Ventures, Y Combinator & Global Founders Capital. We're a team of 150+ across India, the UK, the UAE, EU and the US, working with extreme ownership, radical candour, and an obsession with customer impact.

We celebrate builders who question assumptions, ship fast, and turn regulatory complexity into elegant solutions. If you’re driven to redefine what global banking can be, we’d love to build the future with you.


ABOUT THE ROLE

This role exists to define how Aspora shows up in the world. We're building a new category: modern diaspora banking for people on the move. Someone has to turn complex financial products into narratives that land. That is this role.

You'll own positioning and messaging across our products and corridors. You'll sit with product and growth in Bangalore, close to where decisions get made. You'll shape launches, sharpen our category story, and turn customer insight into differentiated messaging. This is a high-ownership IC role for someone who thinks in narratives and backs them with data.

📍 Bangalore | In-office



WHAT YOU’LL OWN

 - Define positioning and messaging frameworks for Aspora's products across global diaspora corridors

 - Own go-to-market strategy for launches across the UK, UAE, US, Canada and beyond

 - Shape the category narrative that sets Aspora apart in modern diaspora banking

 - Drive customer and competitor insight through research, user interviews and market analysis

 - Partner with product and growth to influence the roadmap

 - Lead messaging consistency across every customer touchpoint, from acquisition to retention
   


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 4+ years in product marketing ideally at an early-stage startup in fintech or a regulated product

 - Strong writer and storyteller

 - Fluent in GTM planning, feature launches and building messaging hierarchies

 - Analytical and insight-driven

 - You read product nuance and user psychology to find the "why it matters"

 - First-principles thinker — you question the default before adopting it
   


BONUS POINTS

 - Fintech or financial services experience

 - 0→1 exposure: defining messaging for products and markets without a playbook
   


WHY JOIN ASPORA?

 - Own the function: product marketing at Aspora is yours to shape. High responsibility, real autonomy, and the room to make this role your own

 - Own the category story for borderless banking and shape how the global diaspora sees us

 - Sit at the intersection of product, growth and brand, with real influence over the roadmap

 - Build 0 to 1: define messaging for products and markets that don't have a playbook yet

 - Go global: work across the UK, UAE, US, Canada and beyond from our Bangalore hub

 - Competitive salary, comprehensive benefits, and ESOPs aligned with Aspora's long-term vision
   

If you have exceptional communication skills, love crafting compelling narratives, and want to help revolutionize financial services for diaspora communities, we'd love to hear from you!

source_jobs.title: Senior iOS Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ASPORA




People on the move deserve a bank that moves with them. Since 2022, Aspora has been building a borderless financial operating system that makes money as mobile and transparent as its users.

Backed by influential venture capitalists like Sequoia Capital, Greylock Partners, Hummingbird Ventures, Y Combinator & Global Founders Capital. We're a team of 150+ across India, the UK, the UAE, EU and the US, working with extreme ownership, radical candour, and an obsession with customer impact.

We celebrate builders who question assumptions, ship fast, and turn regulatory complexity into elegant solutions. If you’re driven to redefine what global banking can be, we’d love to build the future with you.

About the Role :

At Aspora, we’re looking for a sharp, hands-on Senior iOS Engineer to join our team in Bangalore. You’ll be part of the core team shaping our iOS app — a product that brings modern banking to users across the globe.

We’re not chasing buzzwords — we’re looking for strong mobile fundamentals, sound thinking, and clean code. If you enjoy reasoning through complex problems, designing systems that scale, and shipping things that actually matter, you’ll feel right at home here.

You’ll work in a fast-moving, product-focused environment where learning is part of the job — whether that’s Firebase, A/B testing, or a brand-new design system. What matters most to us is your ability to think, build, and grow.

Location: Bangalore l In-office l 5 days week




WHAT YOU’LL OWN:

 - Build and shape the foundation of our iOS app — from architecture decisions to hands-on implementation — with a strong emphasis on code quality, clean design patterns, and maintainability.

 - Lead the full development cycle: from ideation to launch, with deep involvement in feature scoping, design discussions, and post-release iterations.

 - Think deeply, code thoughtfully: We’re looking for someone who understands how things work under the hood — threading, dependency management, scaling architecture — and makes smart technical decisions.

 - Collaborate across functions: Work closely with product managers, designers, and backend engineers to craft intuitive and impactful features that reach a global audience.

 - Drive technical excellence: Champion best practices, raise the bar for the team, and keep us current with the latest in iOS — while staying practical and grounded.

 - Own, learn, and grow: Whether it's picking up Firebase, implementing A/B testing, or solving performance bottlenecks — we value curiosity and the ability to learn what the moment calls for.




WHAT WE NEED :

 - At least 6+ years of hands-on experience building and maintaining iOS applications — you’ve shipped apps, worked with APIs, and handled real-world product and user constraints.

 - Strong command of Swift and SwiftUI, with solid working knowledge of UIKit, Core Data, Core Animation, and Apple’s Human Interface Guidelines.

 - You know your fundamentals — multithreading, memory management, architecture patterns like MVC and MVVM, and data persistence aren't just buzzwords to you; they’re tools you use with intent.

 - Fluency with tools of the trade — Xcode, Git, and whatever else helps you build fast, test thoroughly, and deliver with confidence.

 - You think like an engineer, not just a coder — you break down complex problems, weigh trade-offs, and write clean, maintainable, modular code.

 - Architectural instincts — you’re comfortable designing scalable mobile systems, using dependency injection and modular thinking to keep things tidy and testable.

 - Experience integrating APIs — you're at home working with RESTful endpoints, handling errors gracefully, and building smooth async flows.

 - Debugging and performance matters — you’ve used Instruments or similar tools to profile apps and smooth out bottlenecks before users ever notice them.

 - Curiosity and a builder’s mindset — you’re excited to pick up tools like Firebase, analytics, or A/B tes

source_jobs.title: Founding GTM Specialist (Business Banking)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ASPORA




People on the move deserve a bank that moves with them. Since 2022, Aspora has been building a borderless financial operating system that makes money as mobile and transparent as its users.

Backed by influential venture capitalists like Sequoia Capital, Greylock Partners, Hummingbird Ventures, Y Combinator & Global Founders Capital. We're a team of 150+ across India, the UK, the UAE, EU and the US, working with extreme ownership, radical candour, and an obsession with customer impact.

We celebrate builders who question assumptions, ship fast, and turn regulatory complexity into elegant solutions. If you’re driven to redefine what global banking can be, we’d love to build the future with you.


ABOUT THE ROLE

Aspora is building a business banking platform for SMEs that move money across borders. Logistics companies, import/export businesses, professional services agencies: these are businesses that existing banks have never properly served. This role exists to go get them.

You will be among the first people defining how this product finds its customers. That means building the pipeline, running the full sales cycle, developing partnerships, and feeding what you learn directly back into product. There is no inherited playbook. You write it.

You will work inside a small team of ex-founders and high-agency operators. This team exists to launch products that weren't supposed to be possible.

Location: Dubai, UAE. This role is in-office and requires UAE residency. Candidates must already be based in Dubai.




WHAT YOU'LL OWN

 - Build and own the GTM playbook for Aspora's business banking platform in the UAE: from zero to first customers.

 - Generate pipeline across UAE SMEs through high-volume outbound, discovery calls, and prospect relationships with CFOs, finance directors, and treasury heads.

 - Own full-cycle sales from first touch to signed contract, including negotiations, close, and early account relationships.

 - Develop partnership channels that reach UAE SMEs at the moment they need a banking solution: logistics, import/export, professional services.

 - Feed market intelligence back into product: surface what customers actually need, advocate internally, and shape how the platform evolves.




WHO YOU ARE

 - 3-6 years in B2B sales, BD, or partnerships in the UAE, selling financial products to SME finance teams: CFOs, finance directors, or treasury heads.

 - Working knowledge of cross-border payments, FX, or business banking products and the real problems they solve for SMEs.

 - Track record of building pipeline and closing deals in environments where the product and the playbook didn't exist yet.

 - Can operate without a playbook. You write them.

 - Bias for execution: you prospect, qualify, run discovery, and close — and you log what you learned before the day ends.

 - Based in the UAE, with existing presence in its business or financial services ecosystem.

 - Strong advantages: Arabic language skills, a network across UAE logistics, import/export, or professional services sectors.




WHY ASPORA

 - Own the zero-to-one GTM motion for a new business banking product: you will define how it finds its market in the UAE.

 - Work alongside a team of ex-founders and operators who have launched and scaled fintech products across multiple markets.

 - Sell a product that solves a real, decades-old problem: SMEs with cross-border payment flows have been underserved by banks since forever.

 - Build across the full arc of a fintech product launch: ICP discovery, channel development, design partnerships, and scaled growth.

 - Competitive salary, comprehensive benefits, and ESOPs aligned with Aspora's long-term vision.

#LI-DNP

source_jobs.title: Lead Product Designer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ASPORA




People on the move deserve a bank that moves with them. Since 2022, Aspora has been building a borderless financial operating system that makes money as mobile and transparent as its users.

Backed by influential venture capitalists like Sequoia Capital, Greylock Partners, Hummingbird Ventures, Y Combinator & Global Founders Capital. We're a team of 150+ across India, the UK, the UAE, EU and the US, working with extreme ownership, radical candour, and an obsession with customer impact.

We celebrate builders who question assumptions, ship fast, and turn regulatory complexity into elegant solutions. If you’re driven to redefine what global banking can be, we’d love to build the future with you.




ABOUT THE ROLE



As Lead Product Designer for Aspora you will be responsible for taking Aspora’s international fintech platform to the next level in both user experience and visual design. You will lead the product design process from early-stage user research, through to the delivery of polished, production-ready experiences. You will own and evolve our design system, help to guide the craft and growth of other designers, and work hand-in-hand with Product, Engineering, and Growth teams in a fast-moving and often ambiguous environment.



This role is both strategic and hands-on, requiring you to set a clear vision for design while also delivering high-quality work yourself.




WHAT YOU’LL OWN



 - Lead end-to-end product design processes: research, wireframing, high-fidelity mocks and prototypes.

 - Shapen and scale our design system, enabling high execution speed whilst ensuring consistency, accessibility, and efficiency.

 - Mentor and support a small team of designers to foster craft, growth, and collaboration.

 - Collaborate deeply with our Product leaders and Engineers to define user-centered features and drive clear flows.

 - Advocate for user-experience best practices through user interviews, usability testing, and design critique rituals.

 - Set and uphold design standards across mobile (iOS/Android) and web platforms.

 - Balance strategic vision with hands-on execution. Own high-impact design initiatives from concept through launch.




WHO YOU ARE



 - 7+ years of product design experience. Ideally within consumer-facing platforms.

 - Proven leadership in scaling design systems and mentoring junior designers.

 - Mastery in tools like Figma (or similar), prototyping, and responsive design principles.

 - Strong UX fundamentals and ability to translate research insights into elegant, human-centered designs.

 - Skilled collaborator who works effectively with Product, Engineering, and any other relevant stakeholders in a dynamic cross-functional environment.

 - Comfortable navigating ambiguity with high ownership, whilst optimising for speed.




WHY ASPORA?



 - Shape the future of a global fintech platform and redefine banking for immigrants worldwide.

 - Take ownership of building a world class Product Design engine whilst personally owning delivery for varied and interesting projects.

 - Be part of an early-stage team where your work will have a significant impact.

 - Work in a dynamic, international and fast-paced environment that encourages innovation and growth.

 - Collaborate with passionate individuals who are committed to building world-class products.

 - Competitive salary, comprehensive benefits, and ESOPs aligned with Aspora’s long-term vision.



#LI-AR1

source_jobs.title: Engineering Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ASPORA




People on the move deserve a bank that moves with them. Since 2022, Aspora has been building a borderless financial operating system that makes money as mobile and transparent as its users.

Backed by influential venture capitalists like Sequoia Capital, Greylock Partners, Hummingbird Ventures, Y Combinator & Global Founders Capital. We're a team of 150+ across India, the UK, the UAE, EU and the US, working with extreme ownership, radical candour, and an obsession with customer impact.

We celebrate builders who question assumptions, ship fast, and turn regulatory complexity into elegant solutions. If you’re driven to redefine what global banking can be, we’d love to build the future with you.


WHAT YOU WILL DO

 1. Team & Technical Leadership

 - Lead, mentor, and support engineers while setting clear technical direction for the pod.

 - Build a high-ownership, growth-oriented culture through effective feedback, coaching, and collaboration.

 - Contribute to key architectural discussions, technical decisions, and hands-on problem-solving with senior engineers.
   

 2. Cross-Functional Collaboration

 - Work closely with Product to challenge requirements, drive clarity, and ensure alignment on priorities.

 - Partner with Support & Operations to understand customer issues and operational realities, feeding these insights into planning and improvements.

 - Communicate progress, risks, and decisions transparently with stakeholders and leadership.
   

 3. Execution, Delivery & Ownership

 - Drive end-to-end execution by planning, prioritising, and tracking engineering work.

 - Maintain strong development velocity by removing blockers and enabling smooth workflows.

 - Uphold high engineering standards and ensure the pod builds reliable, well-tested, and operationally sound systems.

 - Own pod-level metrics (e.g., latency, success rates, efficiency) and use data-driven insights to continuously refine execution and delivery.
   
   


WHO YOU ARE

 - 6–9 years of experience in software engineering.

 - Proven track record of transitioning from individual contributor to people manager.

 - Thrives in fast-paced, high-growth startup environments.

 - Combines hands-on technical expertise with effective team leadership and mentorship.

 - You excel at making decisions that serve business goals while nurturing the development of your people.

 - We are language-agnostic and value strong engineering fundamentals over specific tech stacks.




WHY ASPORA?

 - Lead some of the most critical pods with direct impact on customer trust and business growth.

 - Blend hands-on engineering with leadership—ideal for someone who enjoys both building and shaping teams.

 - High ownership, autonomy, and the opportunity to define engineering culture from the ground up.

 - Work in a fast-paced environment with sharp cross-functional partners and supportive leadership.




PERKS AND BENEFITS

 - Competitive compensation and equity, aligned with experience, impact and market standards.

 - Learning & development support, including professional development budgets and learning stipends.

 - Wellness and team engagement programs to support work-life integration and collaboration.

source_jobs.title: Head of Design
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ASPORA




People on the move deserve a bank that moves with them. Since 2022, Aspora has been building a borderless financial operating system that makes money as mobile and transparent as its users.

Backed by influential venture capitalists like Sequoia Capital, Greylock Partners, Hummingbird Ventures, Y Combinator & Global Founders Capital. We're a team of 150+ across India, the UK, the UAE, EU and the US, working with extreme ownership, radical candour, and an obsession with customer impact.

We celebrate builders who question assumptions, ship fast, and turn regulatory complexity into elegant solutions. If you’re driven to redefine what global banking can be, we’d love to build the future with you.



About the Role

This role exists to unify Aspora's entire design output under a single vision. Product, brand, and marketing design have each been built independently. That era ends here.

As Head of Design, you'll lead three design disciplines: product design, brand design, and marketing design. You'll set the direction, build the team, and ensure everything we put in front of customers — from onboarding flows to campaign creative — is coherent, considered, and world-class.

You'll partner directly with Product, Engineering, Growth, and Marketing leadership. Roughly 60% of your time is leadership and direction-setting; 40% is hands-on, in the Product Design domain.

 
 

What You'll Own

 - Lead and scale all three design functions: product design, brand design, and marketing design, including hiring, structure, and culture.

 - Define Aspora's unified design vision across every surface: app, web, brand identity, and growth creative.

 - Drive the evolution of Aspora's design system so it serves both product and marketing teams without friction.

 - Shape cross-functional design direction alongside Product, Engineering, and Growth, from early concept through to launch.

 - Establish design quality standards, critique culture, and operating rhythm across all three disciplines.

 - Own Aspora's brand integrity globally, ensuring visual and tonal consistency across all markets and channels.

 
 

Who You Are

 - 8+ years in design with 3+ years in leadership, with hands-on experience spanning product design and at least one of brand or marketing design.

 - Built and led multi-disciplinary design teams. You know how to structure for scale, not just for now.

 - Strong product design foundation: UX flows, interaction design, high-fidelity UI, and design systems (Figma or equivalent).

 - Fluent in brand and marketing design: visual identity, campaign creative, and the discipline of making both feel like one thing.

 - High ownership. You treat problems as yours until they're solved. You don't wait for a brief to make a call.

 - Comfortable with ambiguity. You build structure, not wait for it.

 - Strong advantage: experience in fintech, regulated industries, or multi-market consumer products.

 
 

Why Aspora

 - Build the design function that defines how a global fintech looks, feels, and behaves, across every product surface and market.

 - Own the full design remit from day one: product, brand, and marketing — rare scope at this stage of company.

 - Work at the intersection of design, growth, and product in a team shipping real products to real users across multiple geographies.

 - Shape how design earns its seat at the table in a company where execution is currency and craft is taken seriously.

 - Competitive salary, comprehensive benefits, and ESOPs aligned with Aspora's long-term vision.

source_jobs.title: Treasury Operations Lead
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ASPORA




People on the move deserve a bank that moves with them. Since 2022, Aspora has been building a borderless financial operating system that makes money as mobile and transparent as its users.

Backed by influential venture capitalists like Sequoia Capital, Greylock Partners, Hummingbird Ventures, Y Combinator & Global Founders Capital. We're a team of 150+ across India, the UK, the UAE, EU and the US, working with extreme ownership, radical candour, and an obsession with customer impact.

We celebrate builders who question assumptions, ship fast, and turn regulatory complexity into elegant solutions. If you’re driven to redefine what global banking can be, we’d love to build the future with you.


ABOUT THE ROLE

Treasury Operations is the function that keeps every dollar, dirham, and pound moving reliably. This role exists to run it. We build payment and treasury infrastructure that is fast, transparent, and trusted — across fiat, digital assets, and complex cross-border corridors. Treasury is central to this mission: it’s the system that ensures every promise we make to customers is operationally sound, liquid, and scalable.

We are looking for a Treasury Operations Lead who combines operational precision with a builder’s mindset. This role is ideal for someone who wants to develop deep expertise in multi-currency treasury operations, settlements, and liquidity management, and use that expertise to help shape how Aspora scales globally.

You will own intraday liquidity, FX execution, foreign inward remittance operations, and the working capital discipline that underpins corridor performance. You will work closely with the Head of Treasury and Finance to keep positions tight, reconciliations clean, and processes built to scale.

This is a hands-on role. You are in the positions, in the models, and in the room when decisions get made.




WHAT YOU’LL OWN

 - Own intraday cash positioning across all nostro and partner accounts — real-time, accurate, and acted on without escalation

 - Drive foreign inward remittance operations end-to-end: receipt, attribution, reconciliation, and SLA performance across SWIFT, SEPA, and local rails

 - Execute FX across a multi-currency book, managing exposure from inflows, settlement mismatches, and corridor-level timing asymmetries

 - Build and maintain the working capital model: prefunding schedules, corridor utilisation, funding cost per corridor, and capital cycle tracking

 - Own reconciliation accuracy and exception management: identify breaks early, resolve fast, and close the loop on every open item


WHO YOU ARE

 - 5+ years in Treasury Operations, Liquidity Management, or FX — at a fintech, bank, or high-volume cross-border business

 - You have personally run inward remittance operations across multiple corridors or currencies, in a regulated environment

 - You have made live intraday funding decisions on real positions: prefunding, sweeps, inter-account movements

 - Comfortable working across multi-jurisdiction frameworks: RBI/IFSC, UAE Central Bank, FCA, or equivalent

 - High ownership. You treat open positions and unresolved breaks as your problem until they are closed

 - Bias for execution: you build clean processes and document them so the next person doesn't need to ask you


WHY JOIN ASPORA?

 - Direct ownership of full flow of treasury operations — with real accountability from day one

 - Exposure across corridors that matter: UK, UAE, EU, India — multi-currency, multi-rail, regulated at every level

 - Your execution quality directly shapes settlement reliability and Aspora's reputation with banking partners

 - Work alongside a small, senior team building the treasury function the right way — no bureaucracy, maximum impact

 - Competitive salary, comprehensive benefits, and ESOPs aligned with Aspora's long-term vision.

source_jobs.title: Android Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ASPORA




People on the move deserve a bank that moves with them. Since 2022, Aspora has been building a borderless financial operating system that makes money as mobile and transparent as its users.

Backed by influential venture capitalists like Sequoia Capital, Greylock Partners, Hummingbird Ventures, Y Combinator & Global Founders Capital. We're a team of 150+ across India, the UK, the UAE, EU and the US, working with extreme ownership, radical candour, and an obsession with customer impact.

We celebrate builders who question assumptions, ship fast, and turn regulatory complexity into elegant solutions. If you’re driven to redefine what global banking can be, we’d love to build the future with you.


ABOUT THE ROLE

This role exists to build the mobile experience that millions of NRI users trust with their money. Android at Aspora isn't a feature factory. It's where the product becomes real: cross-border transfers, compliance-heavy onboarding, and real-time financial data, all inside a single app.

You'll work directly with product and design to take features from concept to production. You'll own entire surfaces, not just tickets. The bar is high: performance, reliability, and test coverage are non-negotiable in a regulated fintech at this scale.

Location: Bangalore In-office, 5 days a week.



WHAT YOU'LL OWN

 - Build and ship Kotlin-first features across the full Android stack, from UI to data layer, for a global NRI banking app

 - Own the architecture: define and enforce MVVM/MVI patterns, Clean Architecture, and dependency injection using Hilt across the mobile codebase

 - Drive app performance: implement Baseline Profiles, R8/ProGuard, and structured concurrency to keep the experience fast on low-end devices

 - Deliver test coverage across unit and integration layers using JUnit, MockK, Turbine, and Roborazzi with no corner-cutting

 - Shape the offline-first data strategy: design Room schemas, manage Coroutine-based concurrency, and ensure reliability in low-connectivity environments
   


WHO YOU ARE

 - 4–6 years in native Android development, ideally at a fintech, payments, or consumer startup where reliability and quality were table stakes.

 - Fluent in Kotlin: Coroutines, Flow, Jetpack Compose, and the full Jetpack ecosystem are how you think, not what you look up

 - Know your architecture: MVVM, MVI, Clean Architecture, and Repository pattern are defaults, not aspirations

 - Strong on fundamentals: REST via Retrofit and OkHttp, offline-first storage, real-time polling, and push notifications with FCM

 - High ownership. You treat a production bug at 2am as your problem, not the on-call's

 - Bias for execution: you ship, learn, and iterate. You don't wait for perfect specs to start building

 - Strong advantage: Kotlin Multiplatform (KMM) experience. Familiarity with Roborazzi for screenshot testing is a plus.
   


WHY ASPORA

 - Build the Android app that defines how millions of NRIs move and manage money globally: your architecture decisions ship to production and matter at scale

 - Work directly with founding engineers and product leaders: no layers, full context, and real influence over technical direction

 - Gain deep exposure to regulated fintech: cross-border compliance, multi-currency flows, and real-time financial data across jurisdictions

 - Own your roadmap: Aspora engineers define the engineering approach, not just execute it. You'll write the playbook, not follow one

 - Competitive salary, comprehensive benefits, and ESOPs aligned with Aspora's long-term vision
   


INTERVIEW PROCESS

 1. Resume Deep Dive & Core Tech Competencies - 60 mins

 2. Machine Coding: Quality & Trade-offs - 3 hours

 3. Team Fitment Round - 60 mins

 4. Cultural Fitment with Founder - 30 mins

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer - Backend
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ASPORA




People on the move deserve a bank that moves with them. Since 2022, Aspora has been building a borderless financial operating system that makes money as mobile and transparent as its users.

Backed by influential venture capitalists like Sequoia Capital, Greylock Partners, Hummingbird Ventures, Y Combinator & Global Founders Capital. We're a team of 150+ across India, the UK, the UAE, EU and the US, working with extreme ownership, radical candour, and an obsession with customer impact.

We celebrate builders who question assumptions, ship fast, and turn regulatory complexity into elegant solutions. If you’re driven to redefine what global banking can be, we’d love to build the future with you.

About the Role

As a Senior Backend Engineer, you will be responsible for designing, developing, and optimizing scalable backend systems that power Aspora’s fintech platform. You will be an early shaper of the design and architecture of the core product & platform, and work closely with the founding team to deliver 10x value to our users.

As an early-stage startup, we pride ourselves on our ability to roll up our sleeves and build from scratch. We will be shipping code frequently and learning as we go while satisfying all critical security, performance, and uptime requirements for our clients.

We all love to build, scale, and grow: Our team has experience building products, scaling teams, and optimizing for growth while working with ambitious engineers. If you’re passionate about building products that make a complex problem easy this job is for you.

You’ll have the opportunity to shape both the product and the engineering culture. You should be ready to move fast, embrace uncertainty, and wear different hats.

What You’ll Own

 - Design & Develop scalable backend systems that power Aspora’s fintech products.

 - Hands-on Coding—write clean, efficient, and maintainable code, ensuring high performance and security.

 - Optimize for Scale—build resilient, fault-tolerant systems that handle millions of transactions.

 - Collaborate Closely with product, frontend, and infrastructure teams to deliver seamless experiences.

 - Ensure High Availability—implement robust API architectures, distributed systems, and database optimizations.

 - Improve Performance & Reliability—monitor system performance, troubleshoot issues, and optimize backend processes.

 - Prioritize Business Needs—align engineering efforts with business priorities, ensuring rapid iteration and execution.

 - Own System Design—architect backend services that are modular, extensible, and scalable.

What We Need

 - 6-10 years in backend development, preferably in a product-based startup or fintech company.

 - Strong ability to design modular, scalable, and high-performing backend systems.

 - Ability to write efficient, high-performance code that scales.

 - Ability to prioritize product and business needs while making tech decisions.

 - Someone who thrives in early-stage environments, enjoys building things from scratch, and takes high ownership.

 - Strong experience in building RESTful APIs and microservices architectures.

Who You Are

 - You are excited by what we're building at Aspora

 - You thrive in environments that afford you high levels of responsibility and autonomy

 - You hold yourself and those you work with to high standards

 - You are driven to fulfil your potential and grow as an engineer

 - You take the initiative to learn new skills required to complete tasks

 - You behave like an owner, taking total responsibility for your work and considering the bigger picture

 - You are results-oriented and avoid unnecessary tasks

 - You have strong opinions, weakly held, and the humility to change your mind when presented with new evidence

Why Join Aspora?

 - Work on a high-impact product that is redefining banking for immigrants worldwide.

 - Own backend design and execution, solving complex engineering problems at scale.

 - Work alongsi

source_jobs.title: Senior Data Platform Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ASPORA




People on the move deserve a bank that moves with them. Since 2022, Aspora has been building a borderless financial operating system that makes money as mobile and transparent as its users.

Backed by influential venture capitalists like Sequoia Capital, Greylock Partners, Hummingbird Ventures, Y Combinator & Global Founders Capital. We're a team of 150+ across India, the UK, the UAE, EU and the US, working with extreme ownership, radical candour, and an obsession with customer impact.

We celebrate builders who question assumptions, ship fast, and turn regulatory complexity into elegant solutions. If you’re driven to redefine what global banking can be, we’d love to build the future with you.

About the Role

We're building the data infrastructure that powers decisions across every part of our business — from real-time analytics to large-scale batch computation. As a Senior Data Platform Engineer, you'll own the systems that process billions of events, move data reliably, and make insights fast to produce.

You'll work closely with analytics, ML, and product engineering teams — setting the bar for reliability, performance, and data quality across the platform.



What You'll Do

 1. Big Data Platform & Infrastructure

 - Design, build, and operate large-scale data processing infrastructure using Spark on Databricks — ensuring reliability, performance, and cost efficiency at scale.

 - Architect and maintain lakehouse solutions (Delta Lake, Iceberg) including partitioning strategies, Z-ordering, and compaction jobs.

 - Own cluster management, autoscaling policies, and resource governance across Databricks workspaces.

 - Drive platform-level improvements: query optimisation, caching strategies, compute–storage separation, and shuffle tuning.

 2. ETL / ELT Pipeline Engineering

 - Design and build robust, idempotent, and testable data pipelines handling batch and near-real-time workloads.

 - Manage and extend our Airflow-based orchestration layer — DAG authoring standards, dependency management, alerting, and SLA enforcement.

 - Implement and maintain CDC pipelines (Debezium, Kafka Connect, or native DB replication) ensuring low-latency, high-fidelity data propagation.

 - Define data pipeline contracts (schemas, SLAs, quality assertions) and enforce them via automated data quality frameworks.

 3. Analytical Storage & Computation

 - Model and manage analytical data stores — dimensional models, OBT patterns, and aggregation layers optimised for BI and self-serve analytics.

 - Own the evolution of our analytical warehouse/lakehouse stack — performance benchmarking, cost modelling, and technology selection.

 - Build and maintain efficient data serving layers for dashboards, ML feature stores, and reverse ETL use cases.

 - Implement data retention, archival, and lifecycle management policies across hot/warm/cold storage tiers.

 4. Platform Engineering & Developer Experience

 - Define and enforce data platform engineering best practices — code standards, CI/CD for pipelines, automated testing, and observability.

 - Build internal tooling and libraries that make data engineers faster: reusable Spark utilities, pipeline templates, local dev environments.

 - Champion data reliability engineering: lineage tracking, incident response playbooks, pipeline SLO monitoring, and root cause analysis.



Tech-Stack

| Area | Tools | Compute | Apache Spark, Databricks, PySpark, Scala | Orchestration | Apache Airflow, dbt | Ingestion & CDC | Debezium, Kafka, Kafka Connect | Storage | Delta Lake, Iceberg, S3/GCS, Snowflake | Languages | Python, SQL, Scala | Observability | Great Expectations, OpenLineage, Monte Carlo |



What We're Looking For

 - 5+ years of data engineering experience with 2+ years on large-scale big data platforms.

 - Hands-on expertise with Apache Spark — performance tuning, partitioning, broadcast joins, execution plans.

 - Deep Databricks experience — workspace configuration, Unity Catalog, De

source_jobs.title: Chief Compliance Officer (EMI)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ASPORA




People on the move deserve a bank that moves with them. Since 2022, Aspora has been building a borderless financial operating system that makes money as mobile and transparent as its users.

Backed by influential venture capitalists like Sequoia Capital, Greylock Partners, Hummingbird Ventures, Y Combinator & Global Founders Capital. We're a team of 150+ across India, the UK, the UAE, EU and the US, working with extreme ownership, radical candour, and an obsession with customer impact.

We celebrate builders who question assumptions, ship fast, and turn regulatory complexity into elegant solutions. If you’re driven to redefine what global banking can be, we’d love to build the future with you.


ABOUT THE ROLE

This role exists to own Aspora's full compliance mandate for its Lithuanian EMI licence. Compliance at Aspora is not a back-office function. It is a core part of how we operate, expand, and earn trust.

You will be the primary face to the Bank of Lithuania and the internal authority on what the business can and cannot do. That means setting the framework, testing the controls, and advising directly on new products and partnerships before they launch.

You will work closely with the CEO, Management Board, and MLRO.




WHAT YOU'LL OWN

 - Own the full compliance mandate for Aspora's Lithuanian EMI: monitoring adherence to EMD2, PSD2, AML/CTF, sanctions, and consumer protection requirements end-to-end.

 - Lead the supervisory relationship with the Bank of Lithuania: examinations, information requests, and all regulatory correspondence.

 - Build and maintain the internal compliance framework: policies, procedures, controls, and testing across governance, consumer protection, and conduct of business.

 - Drive risk-based compliance reviews and thematic checks to stress-test controls including AML/CTF, sanctions, and outsourcing arrangements, and lead remediation where gaps surface.

 - Advise the CEO, Management Board, and business units on compliance implications of new products, partnerships, and market expansion decisions.

 - Build compliance culture across the organisation through training programmes, incident investigation, and board-level reporting on breaches and remediation.




WHO YOU ARE

 - Significant compliance experience at an EMI, payment institution, bank, or regulated fintech: ideally with direct exposure to the Bank of Lithuania or another EU competent authority.

 - Deep working knowledge of EMD2, PSD2, the Lithuanian Law on Electronic Money and EMIs, AML/CTF, sanctions, and EBA guidelines on outsourcing.

 - Have owned regulator relationships directly: examinations, supervisory correspondence, and information requests under your name.

 - Can move between strategic board-level advice and hands-on control testing without losing either.

 - High ownership. You treat compliance gaps as yours until they are closed.

 - Suitable for fit and proper assessment and approval by the Bank of Lithuania as a key function holder.

 - Fluent Lithuanian and English required. A compliance or AML certification (CAMS, ICA) is a strong advantage.


WHY ASPORA

 - Own the compliance infrastructure for Aspora's EU regulated entity: this is not an inherited, mature framework. You define it.

 - Be the compliance lead at the intersection of a global fintech expanding its regulated European footprint — your decisions directly shape what Aspora can build and where.

 - Work directly with the CEO and Board on compliance strategy, without layers in between.

 - Operate across jurisdictions as Aspora scales its regulated presence across the EU and beyond.

 - Competitive salary, comprehensive benefits, and ESOPs aligned with Aspora's long-term vision.
   
   
   #LI-DNP

source_jobs.title: Operations Lead
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ASPORA




People on the move deserve a bank that moves with them. Since 2022, Aspora has been building a borderless financial operating system that makes money as mobile and transparent as its users.

Backed by influential venture capitalists like Sequoia Capital, Greylock Partners, Hummingbird Ventures, Y Combinator & Global Founders Capital. We're a team of 150+ across India, the UK, the UAE, EU and the US, working with extreme ownership, radical candour, and an obsession with customer impact.

We celebrate builders who question assumptions, ship fast, and turn regulatory complexity into elegant solutions. If you’re driven to redefine what global banking can be, we’d love to build the future with you.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are hiring one Product Operations Lead per product line: Remittance, Banking and Wealth. Each role owns the operational health of that surface end-to-end. This is not a coordination function. You define what a defect is, instrument it, and own the number.

The surfaces are distinct. Remittance means dozens of corridors, payout banks, and AML regimes where a delayed transfer is a family waiting. Banking means card networks, BaaS partners, and chargeback flows. Wealth includes Gold and Equities. Gold means live spot pricing, physical custody, and jurisdiction-by-jurisdiction regulatory classification. Equities means settlement windows, custody reconciliations, and corporate actions where every misstep is monetary and direct.

 


WHAT YOU'LL OWN

 - Operational health. Define product-surface defects, build the dashboard or query that tracks them, decompose the metric, and drive it down end to end.

 - Partner performance and commercial levers. Run weekly partner operating cadences, escalate with evidence, and influence SLAs, cost of funds, take-rate, FX spread, fees, routing, and service quality.

 - SOP and process design. Build the onboarding and steady-state operating model for partners, internal teams, support, compliance, finance, and product.

 - Automation. Ship production workflows that reduce manual ops load — LLM workflows, n8n/Zapier-style orchestration, SQL-triggered alerts, internal tools, and exception-handling systems. The bar is working automation with fallback and measurement, not clever prompts.

 - Data fluency. Write SQL, inspect event or transaction-level data, and stand up your own dashboard in tools like Metabase, Hex, Sigma, or equivalent.

 - Pod operating rhythm. Bring a number, a diagnosis, and a plan to pod reviews. Make trade-offs explicit when everything cannot be fixed at once.

 


WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE

In 90 days: You know the product surface at transaction/event level, own the defect dashboard, have identified the top 2–3 defect drivers, and have shipped at least one measurable process or automation improvement.

In 6 months: You have reduced a priority defect metric, changed partner behaviour through evidence-led escalation, improved at least one SLA or cost/reliability lever, and removed a recurring manual ops workflow.

 


YOU MIGHT BE A STRONG FIT IF

 - You have owned an operational metric, not just supported one.

 - You can explain the mechanics behind a metric movement without hiding behind frameworks.

 - You have worked with external partners — banks, fintech infrastructure providers, custodians, brokers, or regulated operating partners.

 - You can write SQL or are strong enough with data to become self-sufficient quickly.

 - You have shipped automation that real users or internal teams depended on.

 - You are comfortable doing unglamorous work when that is what moves the number.

 


THIS IS PROBABLY NOT FOR YOU IF

 - You want to advise but not operate.

 - You need analytics, product, or engineering to define every problem before you move.

 - Your automation experience is mostly prompts, demos, or hackathons with no production adoption.

 - You are uncomfortable pushing partners with data and asking for named owners, dates, and commitments.

source_jobs.title: Training & Quality Lead
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ASPORA




People on the move deserve a bank that moves with them. Since 2022, Aspora has been building a borderless financial operating system that makes money as mobile and transparent as its users.

Backed by influential venture capitalists like Sequoia Capital, Greylock Partners, Hummingbird Ventures, Y Combinator & Global Founders Capital. We're a team of 150+ across India, the UK, the UAE, EU and the US, working with extreme ownership, radical candour, and an obsession with customer impact.

We celebrate builders who question assumptions, ship fast, and turn regulatory complexity into elegant solutions. If you’re driven to redefine what global banking can be, we’d love to build the future with you.


ABOUT THE ROLE

As Training & Quality Lead, you will own Aspora's end-to-end learning and quality assurance function building the capability infrastructure that powers a world-class CX operation. You'll be the strategic bridge between people development and operational excellence, translating quality signals into training interventions and ensuring every customer interaction reflects Aspora's standard of care.

This is a high-ownership role for someone who thrives at the intersection of data, people, and process — and wants to shape what great looks like in a fast-scaling fintech.

Location: Bangalore, India

Employment Type: Full-time

Work Model: On-site



WHAT YOU'LL DO

Training

 - Design, own, and continuously improve the end-to-end training lifecycle: onboarding, product training, process training, soft skills, compliance, and refresher programs.

 - Develop training content including SOPs, scripts, e-learning modules, role-plays, and assessments aligned to product and regulatory updates.

 - Manage new hire induction and certification programs to ensure readiness before live customer handling.

 - Conduct Training Needs Analysis (TNA) in partnership with quality audit data, CSAT trends, and ORM signals from Locobuzz.

 - Build capability maps for CX teams and design Individual Development Plans (IDPs) for high-potential agents.

 - Evaluate training effectiveness through knowledge checks, post-training assessments, and 30-60-90 day performance tracking.

Quality Governance

 - Own and evolve the Quality Evaluation Framework (QEF) across all interaction channels — voice, chat, email, and digital.

 - Drive calibration sessions with Quality Auditors, team leaders, and operations managers to maintain scoring consistency.

 - Review audit findings and translate patterns into structured coaching interventions, process fixes, and training sprints.

 - Publish monthly T&Q scorecards for leadership covering quality scores, training completion, certification rates, and improvement trends.

 - Partner with compliance and legal teams to embed regulatory requirements into QEF parameters and training content.

 - Define and track KPIs for the Quality Auditor team — audit throughput, calibration accuracy, and coaching effectiveness — via Kapture.

People Management & Leadership

 - Manage, mentor, and develop the Quality Auditor team — set individual performance goals, conduct regular 1:1s, and run periodic appraisal reviews.

 - Build a culture of continuous improvement within the team: celebrate wins, address performance gaps early, and create growth pathways for auditors.

 - Define team-level SOPs, audit playbooks, and knowledge repositories to ensure standardisation and reduce key-person dependency.

 - Identify and groom high-potential team members for expanded responsibilities within the T&Q function.

 - Act as a strategic partner to Operations, Product, and CX leadership on quality and capability roadmaps.

 - Represent the T&Q function in business reviews, regulatory audits, and internal governance forums.


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 4–8 years of experience in Training & Quality in fintech, BFSI, or banking with at least 2–3 years in a people management role directly overseeing a QA or training team.

 - Proven a

source_jobs.title: Customer Support Associate
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ASPORA




People on the move deserve a bank that moves with them. Since 2022, Aspora has been building a borderless financial operating system that makes money as mobile and transparent as its users.

Backed by influential venture capitalists like Sequoia Capital, Greylock Partners, Hummingbird Ventures, Y Combinator & Global Founders Capital. We're a team of 150+ across India, the UK, the UAE, EU and the US, working with extreme ownership, radical candour, and an obsession with customer impact.

We celebrate builders who question assumptions, ship fast, and turn regulatory complexity into elegant solutions. If you’re driven to redefine what global banking can be, we’d love to build the future with you.


CUSTOMER SUPPORT AGENT

Location: In-Person, Bangalore
Shift: Rotational Shift (including night shift), 6-day work week
Duration: Full-time
Requirement: Great communication skills - written and verbal. Immediate joiners preferred

 


ABOUT THE ROLE

At Aspora, we are committed to creating a lasting, positive experience for our global user base. As a Customer Support Intern, you will be the vital link connecting Vance with its valued users. Your role will ensure every interaction is clear, warm, and efficiently resolves problems, building trust and solidifying Vance as a dependable financial partner.

 


WHAT YOU’LL OWN

 - User Support: Serve as the primary contact for users across various channels (chat, E-mail, calls), managing queries, resolving issues, and ensuring satisfaction.

 - Cross-functional Collaboration: Work with internal teams to address user concerns, gather feedback, and streamline processes for timely and accurate resolutions.

 - Feedback Loop: Identify common user issues and contribute to preemptive solutions, enhancing our product and overall user experience.
   
    


WHAT WE NEED

 - 3 to 5 years of experience in a customer-centric role, preferably within the Fintech or Payments industry.

 - Exceptional communication skills, ensuring clarity, warmth, and understanding in every interaction.

 - A proactive mindset, continuously seeking ways to improve user experience and preemptively solve issues.

 - Ability to work collaboratively with internal teams and external partners.
   
    


WHO YOU ARE

 - A customer advocate who understands the importance of relationship-building and efficient problem-solving in customer support.

 - Self-driven and adaptable, capable of managing multiple responsibilities in a fast-paced startup environment.

 - Passionate about fintech and eager to play a key role in Vance’s mission and growth trajectory.

 - A team player, ready to collaborate and ensure alignment with internal teams for the broader benefit of our user community.
   
    


WHY JOIN ASPORA

 - Impactful Interactions: Every user interaction matters. You'll be building trust and helping users make informed financial decisions.

 - Growth and Learning: Immerse yourself in the fintech domain with regular training sessions, workshops, and collaborative team discussions to foster professional growth.

 - Direct Contribution to Success: Your efforts will enhance user satisfaction and retention, playing a critical role in Vance's success.

 - Collaborative Culture: Work in a dynamic team where your feedback is valued and your voice is always heard. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to gain insights into various business facets.

 - Opportunity for Advancement: Show exemplary performance and there's potential for vertical growth within the company. We recognize and reward dedication and talent.

 - Inclusive and Diverse Workplace: Vance prides itself on fostering an environment that celebrates diversity and inclusivity. Your unique perspectives and experiences will be valued and respected.

 - Wealth Creation: Participate in our ESOPs program, aligning your growth with Vance’s success.

source_jobs.title: Wealth Ops Analyst
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ASPORA




People on the move deserve a bank that moves with them. Since 2022, Aspora has been building a borderless financial operating system that makes money as mobile and transparent as its users.

Backed by influential venture capitalists like Sequoia Capital, Greylock Partners, Hummingbird Ventures, Y Combinator & Global Founders Capital. We're a team of 150+ across India, the UK, the UAE, EU and the US, working with extreme ownership, radical candour, and an obsession with customer impact.

We celebrate builders who question assumptions, ship fast, and turn regulatory complexity into elegant solutions. If you’re driven to redefine what global banking can be, we’d love to build the future with you.

About the Role


Aspora's Digital Gold product lets NRIs buy, hold and redeem gold across borders. We've launched in the UK and UAE with a partner in each market and we're expanding. You'll own operations for this business end-to-end — no one above you, no one below you.

You keep transactions flowing, reconciliations clean, escalations closed, and partners aligned and you do it AI-first: using AI to process data, automate the repetitive work and run several flows at once. As the business grows, this can grow into a broader role across Wealth and beyond.


Location: Bangalore | In Office

What You’ll Own

 - Run end-to-end operations for Digital Gold across the UK and UAE — order execution, settlement, redemptions and reporting.

 - Reconcile gold holdings and transactions across internal systems and partner platforms; investigate and close breaks fast.

 - Own escalations and user disputes from first flag to root-cause and closure.

 - Be the operational point of contact for the UK/UAE partners and manage partner transactions.

 - Handle chargeback and payment-dispute cases (evidence, representment, prevention) and compliance-linked cases (KYC/AML,jurisdictional requirements) alongside Compliance.

 - Build AI-driven automations and SOPs so all of this scales without adding headcount.

 - Monitor dashboards for anomalies and delays, drive fixes with product and engineering, and stand up operations for new markets as we expand.
   
   
   
   Who You Are
   
   

 - AI-first by default — you already lean on AI (LLMs, automation tools) to process data, draft, and strip out manual steps. This is
   
   the core of the role, not a bonus.

 - Genuine end-to-end ownership — comfortable being the only person on the function, setting your own priorities, and running it like your own business.

 - A track record of owning operations — we care more about what you've actually run and shipped than years on paper.

 - Having run a function solo counts double.

 - Strong with data - SQL and/or spreadsheets, dashboards, and reconciliation logic.

 - Sharp on transaction lifecycles, reconciliations, disputes/chargebacks, and partner coordination.

 - Calm and precise under time-sensitive, money-movement pressure

 - Strong Advantages of exposure to gold, commodities, or precious metals products. Worked across UK and/or UAE financial regulation. Have built your own automations or scripts (even lightweight) to replace manual ops.


Why Join Aspora?

• Own an entire business line end-to-end from day one — real scope, real autonomy.

• Be AI-first for real: you build the automation, you don't just work the queue.

• Clear path to a bigger role across Wealth and other areas as you prove out.

• Two live markets with more coming — you decide how new markets run.

source_jobs.title: Operations Generalist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT ASPORA




People on the move deserve a bank that moves with them. Since 2022, Aspora has been building a borderless financial operating system that makes money as mobile and transparent as its users.

Backed by influential venture capitalists like Sequoia Capital, Greylock Partners, Hummingbird Ventures, Y Combinator & Global Founders Capital. We're a team of 150+ across India, the UK, the UAE, EU and the US, working with extreme ownership, radical candour, and an obsession with customer impact.

We celebrate builders who question assumptions, ship fast, and turn regulatory complexity into elegant solutions. If you’re driven to redefine what global banking can be, we’d love to build the future with you.


ABOUT THE ROLE

Aspora's Remittances business is scaling across corridors, partners, and payment rails, and we are building the operating system for that scale with an AI-first mindset. We are looking for an Operations Generalist who can work close to live payment operations: monitoring flows, investigating exceptions, coordinating with internal and external stakeholders, and improving the workflows that keep customer money moving reliably.



This is not a passive queue-processing role. You will work across payment operations, partner operations, reconciliations, compliance operations, customer-impacting escalations, and data analysis. The right person brings fintech ops experience, comfort with SQL and dashboards, and the curiosity to use AI, automation, and structured data to make repeat work easier and more reliable.

Location: Bangalore




WHAT YOU'LL OWN

 - Run and monitor defined remittance operations workflows across pay-in, payout, KYC/RFI, refunds, disputes, reconciliations, and partner-dependent exceptions.

 - Use dashboards, SQL, and transaction-level data to investigate anomalies, prepare issue notes, identify likely root causes, and escalate with context.

 - Coordinate with banks, payment providers, gateways, Support, Product, Engineering, Compliance, and Finance on defined escalation paths and issue resolution.

 - Maintain and improve runbooks, queue hygiene, exception categories, controls, and operating notes by corridor, partner, and workflow.

 - Identify recurring manual checks or repeat exceptions and help convert them into AI-assisted triage, automation, better monitoring, or product/partner requirements.

 - Support new corridor, rail, partner, and workflow launches by testing operational readiness, monitoring early issues, and documenting learnings.




WHAT WE NEED

 - 2-5 years of experience in fintech, payments, banking operations, wallet operations, marketplace-fintech operations, or another transaction-heavy operating environment.

 - Experience using AI tools, no-code/low-code automation, or lightweight scripts to reduce manual checks, improve exception handling, or make operational workflows more scalable.

 - Working knowledge of payment operations: pay-ins, payouts, settlements, reconciliation, refunds, disputes/chargebacks, KYC/RFI, partner escalations, and incident handling. Cross-border payments experience is a plus.

 - SQL is required: you should be able to query transaction-level data, validate dashboard signals, investigate operational issues, and explain what the data is showing.

 - Comfort working with dashboards, spreadsheets, queues, logs, SOPs, and issue trackers to keep operational work visible and controlled.

 - Experience coordinating with internal teams and external partners, with the communication discipline to follow up clearly and keep issue notes clean.
   
   




WHO YOU ARE

 - A hands-on, AI-curious operator-builder: you are willing to run the workflow today and interested in making it cleaner, faster, or more automated tomorrow.

 - Detail-oriented and reliable when customer money movement, partner reliability, or SLA/TAT is involved.

 - Analytical and curious; you prefer checking dashboards, data, and transaction records over relying on guesses.

 - Orga

notes: Aspora is a fintech startup with a strong engineering focus, backed by influential venture capitalists.
